products.name: Compute Engine (GCE)
products.subtitle: IaaS VMs — full control
products.when: Full OS control, legacy workloads, GPU/TPU, custom VMs
diffs: You manage the OS, patches, and runtime
GPU/TPU support for ML
Preemptible VMs for fault-tolerant workloads (up to 80% off)
Automatic sustained use discounts
Custom machine types (independent vCPU + RAM)
products.aceTip: Know when to use it vs App Engine. GCE = maximum control. Preemptible VMs = up to 91% discount on Spot VMs.

products.name: App Engine (GAE)
products.subtitle: PaaS — deploy apps without managing infrastructure
products.when: Web/API apps that scale automatically without wanting to manage servers
diffs: Standard: scales to zero, support for specific runtimes (Python, Java, Go, PHP, Ruby, Node.js)
Flexible: Docker containers, more runtimes, does not scale to zero
Standard scales faster, Flexible supports any language
Native traffic splitting between versions
products.aceTip: Standard vs Flexible is a classic exam question. Standard = scales to zero, faster instances, fixed runtime. Flexible = Docker, more expensive, does not scale to zero.

products.name: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
products.subtitle: Managed Kubernetes
products.when: Containerized workloads that need orchestration, microservices
diffs: Autopilot: GKE without managing nodes (pay per pod)
Standard: you manage node pools
Native integration with Artifact Registry, Cloud Build, and Cloud IAM
Cluster Autoscaler and Horizontal Pod Autoscaler
Multi-zone and regional clusters for HA
products.aceTip: Autopilot vs Standard: Autopilot = less control, less ops. Know how to configure node pools, autoscaling, and namespaces.

products.name: Cloud Run
products.subtitle: Serverless containers
products.when: Stateless containers that scale to zero, without managing infrastructure
diffs: Based on Knative, scales to zero automatically
Pay per request (100ms granularity)
Cloud Run Jobs for batch workloads
Supports any container that runs on port 8080
Simpler than GKE, more flexible than Cloud Functions
products.aceTip: Choosing between Cloud Run vs Cloud Functions: Run = container in any language, more control. Functions = small event, code without a container.

products.name: Cloud Functions
products.subtitle: FaaS — event-driven functions
products.when: Small code that reacts to events (Pub/Sub, HTTP, Storage, Firestore)
diffs: Gen 1 vs Gen 2 (Gen 2 = backed by Cloud Run, more features, concurrency per instance)
Triggers: HTTP, Pub/Sub, Cloud Storage, Firestore, Firebase
Cold start can be an issue (use min-instances to mitigate)
Limit of 9 min (Gen 1) / 60 min (Gen 2)
products.aceTip: Cold starts are covered in the exam. Min-instances = avoids cold start (at a cost). Gen 2 is preferred for new functions.

label: Storage
desc: Object, block, file, and database storage

products.name: Cloud Storage (GCS)
products.subtitle: Durable and global object storage
products.when: Store blobs, backups, static assets, ML data
diffs: Standard: frequently accessed data
Nearline: accessed less than once a month (30 days min retention)
Coldline: accessed less than once a quarter (90 days min retention)
Archive: accessed less than once a year (365 days min retention, more expensive retrieval)
Lifecycle policies to move between classes automatically
Durability of 99.999999999% (11 nines)
products.aceTip: Questions about storage classes are frequent. Remember the minimum retention (30/90/365 days) and the increasing retrieval cost.

products.name: Persistent Disk (PD)
products.subtitle: Block storage for VMs
products.when: VM boot disks, databases, persistent storage for GKE
diffs: Standard HDD, Balanced (SSD), SSD, Extreme SSD
Regional PD: replicated across 2 zones for HA
Can be resized without downtime
Automatic incremental snapshots
Cross-region snapshot for DR
products.aceTip: Regional PD = HA. Snapshots are incremental (delta only). Extreme PD = high IOPS for critical databases.

products.name: Filestore
products.subtitle: Managed NFS file storage
products.when: Apps that need a shared file system (NFS)
diffs: NFSv3 compatible
Basic HDD/SSD and Enterprise
Enterprise = HA with 99.99% SLA, regional support
Use with GKE via ReadWriteMany volumes
products.aceTip: Filestore = NFS. Use when multiple VMs need to read/write to the same file system.

products.name: Cloud SQL
products.subtitle: Managed MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server
products.when: Relational databases without wanting to manage infrastructure, traditional web/mobile apps
diffs: Supports MySQL 5.7/8.0, PostgreSQL 9.6-15, SQL Server
HA with automatic failover (primary + standby in the same region)
Cross-region read replicas are possible
Automatic backups and PITR (Point-in-Time Recovery)
Limit of ~100 TB
usecases: Any app with a relational database
products.aceTip: Cloud SQL HA = 2 instances in the same region (primary + standby). Cross-region read replicas = disaster recovery.

products.name: Cloud Spanner
products.subtitle: Globally distributed SQL, horizontal scale
products.when: Global apps that need strong consistency AND horizontal scaling
diffs: Only SQL database that scales horizontally (unlike Cloud SQL)
Strong external consistency (TrueTime)
Multi-regional = 99.999% SLA
Much more expensive than Cloud SQL
No practical limit on scale
products.aceTip: Spanner = when you need SQL + global horizontal scale + strong consistency. If the exam mentions 'scaling globally with ACID', the answer is Spanner.

products.name: Cloud Bigtable
products.subtitle: NoSQL wide-column for high throughput
products.when: Time series, IoT data, ML feature store — billions of rows, low latency
diffs: Wide-column store (similar to HBase/Cassandra)
Latency < 10ms for reads/writes
No SQL, no joins, schema-free
Scales to PBs of data
Ideal for time-series and IoT data
products.aceTip: Bigtable ≠ BigQuery. Bigtable = operational, low latency, NoSQL. BigQuery = analytical, OLAP, SQL.

products.name: Firestore
products.subtitle: NoSQL document store for mobile/web
products.when: Mobile/web apps with real-time sync, hierarchical data
diffs: Successor to Datastore (Firestore in Datastore mode = compatible with Datastore)
Native mode: best for mobile, real-time listeners
ACID transactions with optimistic locking
Serverless, scales automatically
Native offline sync (mobile SDK)
products.aceTip: Firestore Native vs Datastore mode are exam questions. Native = real-time, mobile. Datastore mode = compatibility with legacy apps.

products.name: Cloud Memorystore
products.subtitle: Managed Redis and Memcached
products.when: Session caching, lightweight message queues, real-time leaderboards
diffs: Redis: supports persistence, pub/sub, structured data
Memcached: simpler, only key-value caching
Fully managed (no OS ops)
High availability with Redis replication
products.aceTip: Redis vs Memcached: Redis = persistence, complex structures. Memcached = pure cache, horizontal.

label: Networking
desc: VPC, load balancers, CDN, DNS, and connectivity

products.name: VPC (Virtual Private Cloud)
products.subtitle: Global virtual private network
products.when: Always — all GCP infrastructure runs inside a VPC
diffs: VPC in GCP is global (spans all regions, unlike AWS which is regional)
Subnets are regional within the global VPC
VPC Peering to connect VPCs without overlapping CIDRs
Shared VPC to share a VPC across projects
Firewall rules at the instance level (via service accounts or tags)
products.aceTip: Global VPC is a key difference vs AWS. Shared VPC = org that centralizes network. VPC Peering = no overlapping IPs.

products.name: Cloud Load Balancing
products.subtitle: Managed load balancers (L4 and L7)
products.when: Distribute traffic across instances with HA and scalability
diffs: Global External HTTP(S) LB: L7, anycast, SSL, integrated Cloud CDN
Regional External HTTP(S) LB: L7, per region
TCP/UDP Network LB: L4, regional, preserves client IP
Internal LB: private traffic between VMs
Backend services can have health checks, session affinity
products.aceTip: Knowing which LB to use is a frequent topic: Global HTTPS = global web. Network LB = L4, low latency. Internal = private traffic.

products.name: Cloud CDN
products.subtitle: Global CDN with content caching
products.when: Accelerate delivery of static or dynamic content to global users
diffs: Integrated with the Global HTTP(S) LB
Cached at Google's POPs (Points of Presence)
Granular cache invalidation by URL
Signed URLs and Signed Cookies for private content
products.aceTip: CDN = check a box on the Load Balancer. Signed URLs = time-limited access to private objects.

products.name: Cloud DNS
products.subtitle: Low-latency managed DNS
products.when: Host DNS zones, internal and public name resolution
diffs: Public and private zones
Private zones work only within the VPC
DNS peering to resolve names from other VPCs
DNSSEC supported for public zones
100% availability SLA
products.aceTip: Private zones = DNS resolution only within the VPC. Useful for internal service discovery.

products.name: Cloud Interconnect & VPN
products.subtitle: On-premises connectivity
products.when: Connect on-prem data center to GCP securely with low latency
diffs: Cloud VPN: IPsec, up to 3 Gbps per tunnel, public internet, lower cost
Dedicated Interconnect: direct physical connection (10 or 100 Gbps), lower latency
Partner Interconnect: via partner provider, more flexible than Dedicated
Cloud Router with BGP for dynamic routing
HA VPN: 99.99% SLA with 2 gateways + 4 tunnels
products.aceTip: VPN = public internet, cheap, variable latency. Interconnect = physical cable, expensive, predictable latency, high throughput.

products.name: Cloud NAT
products.subtitle: Managed Network Address Translation
products.when: VMs without public IPs need to access the internet (downloads, updates)
diffs: Does not require gateway VMs, fully managed
Scales automatically
Works with specific subnets
Does not support inbound connections (outbound only)
usecases: Private VMs that need internet access
products.aceTip: Cloud NAT = egress to the internet without public IP. Not bidirectional — outbound only.

label: Big Data
desc: Analytics, streaming, data warehouse, and pipelines

products.name: BigQuery
products.subtitle: Serverless and analytical data warehouse
products.when: Analysis of large data volumes, SQL analytics, integrated ML
diffs: Serverless: no clusters to manage
Separation of compute/storage (pay per query or per slot)
On-demand: $5/TB processed. Capacity: slot reservation
BigQuery ML: train ML models via SQL
Streaming ingestion for near-real-time data
Partitioned and clustered tables to reduce query costs
products.aceTip: BigQuery ≠ Bigtable. BQ = OLAP, analytical, batch. Partitioning and clustering reduce cost — a frequent question.

products.name: Pub/Sub
products.subtitle: Global asynchronous messaging
products.when: Decouple producers from consumers, event streaming
diffs: Push (GCP delivers to endpoint) vs Pull (consumer fetches messages)
At-least-once delivery (may duplicate — use IDs for idempotency)
Message retention up to 7 days
Ordering by key with Pub/Sub Ordering Keys
Dead Letter Topics for failed messages
products.aceTip: Pub/Sub is at-least-once, not exactly-once. Push vs Pull: Push = no consumer server always running (good for Functions). Pull = consumer control.

products.name: Dataflow
products.subtitle: Batch and stream data pipelines (Apache Beam)
products.when: Complex ETL, stream processing, large-scale data transformations
diffs: Based on Apache Beam (open source)
Unifies batch and streaming in the same code
Autoscale workers automatically
Windowing functions for stream data (tumbling, sliding, session)
More complex than Dataproc, but serverless
products.aceTip: Dataflow = Apache Beam on GCP. When you see 'process data stream' + BigQuery in the exam, it is usually Dataflow.

products.name: Dataproc
products.subtitle: Managed Hadoop and Spark
products.when: Migrate existing Hadoop/Spark workloads, process data with Spark ecosystem
diffs: Spin up cluster in ~90 seconds (vs hours on-prem)
Ephemeral clusters: create, process, destroy (cost optimized)
Supports Hadoop, Spark, Hive, Pig, Presto
Preemptible VMs on worker nodes to reduce cost
Integrates with GCS as an HDFS replacement
products.aceTip: Dataproc = lift-and-shift of Hadoop/Spark. Dataflow = new pipelines with Beam. Choose Dataproc when you already have Spark code.

products.name: Looker / Looker Studio
products.subtitle: BI and dashboards
products.when: Data visualization, business reporting, executive dashboards
diffs: Looker Studio (formerly Data Studio): free, connects to BigQuery, Sheets, etc.
Looker: enterprise BI, data modeling with LookML, more powerful
Looker Studio = for basic BI. Looker = corporate data governance
products.aceTip: On the ACE, the focus is on BigQuery. Looker/Studio appear as data destinations for visualization.

label: DevOps & IAM
desc: CI/CD, monitoring, IAM, projects, and resource management

products.name: Cloud IAM
products.subtitle: Identity and Access Management
products.when: Control who can do what on which resources
diffs: Principal: Google Account, Service Account, Group, Domain
Role: collection of permissions (Basic, Predefined, Custom)
Basic roles (Owner/Editor/Viewer) are very broad — avoid in production
Service Accounts: identities for workloads (not for people)
Workload Identity Federation: no SA keys for external workloads
Principle of least privilege = use Predefined roles
products.aceTip: IAM is the most tested topic on the ACE. Know the hierarchy: Org > Folder > Project > Resource. Policies are inherited downwards. Deny policies override allows.

products.name: Cloud Monitoring
products.subtitle: Metrics, alerts, and dashboards
products.when: Monitor resource health, define SLOs, receive alerts
diffs: Collects metrics from GCP, AWS, and on-premises (agent)
Alerting Policies with notifications via email, PagerDuty, Slack
Uptime Checks to test external availability
Custom metrics via API or OpenTelemetry
Workspace groups monitoring of multiple projects
products.aceTip: Alerting policies are configured with conditions + notification channels. Uptime checks = external HTTP/TCP verification.

products.name: Cloud Logging
products.subtitle: Centralized logging and auditing
products.when: Centralize application and access audit logs
diffs: Admin Activity logs: always on, free, cannot be disabled
Data Access logs: off by default, generate high volume of data
System Event logs: automatic GCP actions
Log sinks: export logs to GCS, BigQuery, Pub/Sub
Log-based metrics: create metrics from patterns in logs
products.aceTip: Admin Activity = always enabled, immutable. Data Access logs must be explicitly enabled and incur costs. Log sinks = export to BigQuery for analysis.

products.name: Cloud Build
products.subtitle: Serverless CI/CD
products.when: Automate code build, test, and deploy
diffs: Based on container steps (cloudbuild.yaml)
Triggers on push to GitHub, Bitbucket, Cloud Source Repos
Each step runs in an isolated container
Build Cache to speed up repeated builds
Integrated with Artifact Registry to push images
products.aceTip: Cloud Build = serverless CI. Configured by cloudbuild.yaml. Integrates with Artifact Registry to push images.

products.name: Artifact Registry
products.subtitle: Container and package registry
products.when: Store Docker images, npm, Maven, PyPI packages privately
diffs: Replaces legacy Container Registry
Supports Docker, npm, Maven, Python, Go, APT, Yum
Integrated with IAM for fine-grained access control
Integrated vulnerability scanning
Multi-region repositories
products.aceTip: Artifact Registry = modern replacement for Container Registry. Prefer AR for new projects.

products.name: Cloud Deployment Manager
products.subtitle: GCP native IaC (YAML/Jinja/Python)
products.when: Infrastructure as Code with native GCP templates
diffs: YAML + Jinja2 or Python templates
GCP-specific (unlike Terraform which is multi-cloud)
In practice, Terraform is more widely used in the market
Does not require installation of external tools
products.aceTip: ACE mentions Deployment Manager, but Terraform dominates the market. Know that DM uses YAML/Jinja. Terraform uses HCL.

products.name: Secret Manager
products.subtitle: Secrets and credentials management
products.when: Store passwords, API keys, certificates securely
diffs: Secrets versioning (can have multiple versions)
Automatic rotation with Pub/Sub notifications
IAM access with full auditing
Natively integrated with Cloud Run, GKE, App Engine
Backed by KMS encryption
products.aceTip: Secret Manager > environment variables for secrets. Always reference secrets by resource ID, do not hardcode values.

products.name: Cloud KMS
products.subtitle: Cryptographic keys management
products.when: Manage encryption keys, sign data, CMEK for GCP data
diffs: CMEK (Customer Managed Encryption Keys): you control the key
Symmetric (AES-256) and Asymmetric keys (RSA, EC)
HSM keys: hardware security module for compliance
Configurable automatic key rotation
Separation of duties: who holds the key vs who accesses the data
usecases: Encrypt BigQuery/GCS with your own key
products.aceTip: CMEK = you manage the encryption key for GCP data. If the exam says 'control encryption key', the answer is KMS + CMEK.

products.name: VPC Service Controls
products.subtitle: Security perimeter around GCP services
products.when: Prevent data exfiltration, isolate sensitive data from external access
diffs: Defines a perimeter around GCP resources
Blocks access to GCP APIs from outside the perimeter
Ingress/egress rules by IP, identity, service
Different from VPC Firewall: firewall = L4 VM traffic; VPC SC = API access control
products.aceTip: VPC SC ≠ VPC Firewall. Firewall = VM network traffic. VPC SC = GCP API access. To 'prevent access to GCS from outside the perimeter', use VPC SC.

products.name: Cloud Armor
products.subtitle: WAF and DDoS protection
products.when: Protect web apps against DDoS, SQL injection, XSS, bots
diffs: Integrated with the Global HTTP(S) Load Balancer
Pre-configured rules for OWASP Top 10
IP allowlist/denylist by CIDR
Rate limiting by IP
Adaptive Protection: ML to detect DDoS attacks
products.aceTip: Cloud Armor = WAF. Always attach to the Load Balancer. Security policies with priority-based rules.

label: AI / ML
desc: Vertex AI, pre-trained ML APIs, and AI tools

products.name: Vertex AI
products.subtitle: Unified end-to-end ML platform
products.when: Train, tune, host, and monitor custom ML models
diffs: Unifies AutoML + AI Platform in one place
Feature Store: stores and serves features for models
Model Registry: model versioning
Pipelines: training orchestration (based on Kubeflow)
Prediction endpoints with autoscaling
products.aceTip: Vertex AI = complete MLOps platform. AutoML = no-code. Custom training = full control.

products.name: Pre-trained AI APIs
products.subtitle: Vision, NLP, Speech, and Translation
products.when: Add AI capabilities without training models
diffs: Vision AI: detect objects, text (OCR), faces, and labels in images
Natural Language API: sentiment, entities, and syntax in text
Speech-to-Text / Text-to-Speech
Translation API: 100+ languages
Document AI: extract data from PDFs and forms
No training data required
products.aceTip: Pre-trained APIs = zero training. Vertex AutoML = training with your data. Custom training = complete ML code.

products.name: Gemini / Vertex AI Generative
products.subtitle: LLMs and generative models
products.when: Chatbots, text/code/image generation, RAG, agents
diffs: Gemini via Vertex AI or via AI Studio
Grounding with Google Search or own data via RAG
Tuning with your data (supervised fine-tuning)
ACE coverage: Vertex AI Model Garden accesses open-source models
products.aceTip: On the current ACE, GenAI appears more as an architecture question (which service to use) than a technical ML question. Know that Vertex AI is the hub.
